title: Arabic-English Custom Receipt Paper: Artwork and Proofing Checklist
excerpt: A production checklist for GCC brands, POS integrators and distributors managing Arabic-English artwork, variable fields, QR clearance, winding and version approval.
metaDescription: Prepare Arabic-English custom receipt rolls with correct right-to-left text, variable-field clearance, QR spacing, proof control and printer sample approval.
content:
Arabic-English receipt rolls need two approvals: language accuracy and production accuracy. A visually attractive PDF can still fail if Arabic letters disconnect, text direction changes, the print repeat is reversed or fixed artwork overlaps the POS system's variable fields. Use a controlled artwork, proof and device-test workflow before bulk printing.

## Separate Fixed Artwork From Variable POS Content

Start by marking what the paper supplier prints and what the POS system prints at the store.

| Content type | Typical owner | Examples |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Fixed pre-print | Paper converter | Logo, support details, campaign terms, return-policy heading |
| Variable transaction data | POS or invoicing system | Items, price, VAT data, date, transaction number |
| Generated machine element | POS or invoicing system | Transaction-specific QR code or barcode |
| Controlled version content | Buyer and converter | Store group, promotion, language or market version |

Keep a clear variable-print zone. Background tint, lines and decorative elements can reduce readability or interfere with a QR code's quiet zone. Ask the POS provider for the maximum print area and test the final receipt layout.

## Prepare Arabic Text for Production

The buyer should supply final approved Arabic copy or appoint an authorized language reviewer. The converter should not guess translations or reconstruct missing text from an image.

Check these details:

- Right-to-left direction is preserved.
- Arabic letters remain connected correctly.
- Punctuation and parentheses appear in the intended order.
- Arabic and Western numerals follow the buyer's convention.
- English and Arabic baselines align as intended.
- Fonts are licensed, embedded or converted to outlines.
- Small text remains readable at the actual print size.

Review the proof at 100 percent scale and as a complete roll repeat. A cropped screenshot can hide direction and winding errors.

## Define the Printing Specification

The artwork file is only one part of the order. Add colour, side, repeat and registration requirements.

The production brief should state:

1. Roll width, outer diameter, core and winding.
2. Print side: thermal face, back or both.
3. Number of colours and approved colour references.
4. Repeat length and cut or tear relationship.
5. Logo size and position tolerance.
6. Unprinted safe area for variable receipt data.
7. Artwork revision code and version quantity.
8. Packing identification for each version.

For multiple store groups, use a version matrix. Give every design a unique code and show carton quantities by code. This reduces the risk of sending the correct language to the wrong customer or location.

Our [custom-printed thermal paper page](/products/thermal-paper-rolls/custom-printed) explains the main project inputs for branded rolls. Final feasibility depends on artwork, equipment and order quantity.

## Approve a Digital Proof and Physical Sample

The digital proof should show width, print side, unwind direction, repeat boundaries, colour separations and safe areas. The buyer's approval should identify the exact file revision.

Then produce a physical sample or representative proof. Load it into the real printer and print the longest expected receipt. Check:

- Arabic orientation when the receipt exits the device.
- Logo and fixed text position.
- No overlap with variable fields.
- QR or barcode clearance and readability.
- Ink adhesion and absence of transfer or blocking.
- Feed and cut behaviour.
- Appearance after normal handling.

If the final paper, ink, printer or artwork changes, repeat the affected approval. Do not rely on a sample made with an unrelated substrate.

## Protect Artwork Quality Through Repeat Orders

Keep the approved PDF, source file, physical master sample and signed approval together. The purchase order should reference the artwork code, not only the customer name.

Require change notification before substitutions in paper, coating, ink, plate, cylinder, core or packing. For each production batch, retain samples and record the artwork version. This supports investigation if a store reports reversed text, colour variation or scanning problems.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Can the factory translate the Arabic receipt text?

The buyer should provide or approve the translation through a qualified reviewer. The factory can check file integrity and production layout but should not be the sole authority for legal or customer-facing language.

### Should the QR code be pre-printed on the roll?

A transaction-specific QR code is normally generated by the POS or invoicing system. A fixed marketing QR code can be pre-printed when appropriate, but its purpose, destination and position must be clearly separated from invoice data.

### Is a PDF proof enough for bulk approval?

It is necessary but not sufficient for higher-risk projects. A physical sample reveals winding, scale, ink, feeding and interaction with variable printer output.

Upload the artwork, printer model, language versions and quantity through our [contact page](/contact) for a structured pre-production review.

title: Why Thermal Paper Turns Black in Hot Warehouses—and How GCC Buyers Can Reduce the Risk
excerpt: Understand how heat, sunlight, moisture and chemical contact affect thermal paper, then turn GCC storage and transport risks into practical packaging and test requirements.
metaTitle: Thermal Paper Storage for Hot GCC Warehouses
metaDescription: Learn why thermal paper can darken or fade in hot GCC storage and how buyers can specify packaging, warehouse controls, samples and exposure testing.
content:
Thermal paper can darken when enough heat activates its imaging layer before printing. Printed receipts can also lose contrast through prolonged heat, light, moisture or chemical contact. GCC buyers should manage this as an exposure-and-duration problem, then qualify the paper, packing and storage plan for the real logistics route.

## Understand the Three Different Risks

Buyers often combine stock storage, printed-image life and in-use durability into one question. They are separate.

| Risk | Material state | Typical concern |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Unprinted stock storage | Rolls before use | Background darkening, curl or degraded print response |
| Printed-image retention | Completed receipt | Fading, discoloration or loss of contrast |
| In-use durability | Receipt during handling | Heat, oil, water, abrasion or chemical contact |

A grade described as long-life is not automatically water-resistant. A top-coated grade is not automatically suitable for every heat exposure. Request evidence for the specific property and conditions that matter.

## Map the Full GCC Logistics Route

The paper may pass through a factory warehouse, export container, port, inland truck, distributor warehouse, delivery vehicle and retail back room before reaching the printer. Each stage can add heat, moisture or physical damage.

Document:

- Expected shipping season and route.
- Container type and loading duration.
- Pallet position and protection.
- Warehouse ventilation or air conditioning.
- Maximum planned storage period.
- Vehicle or outdoor storage after distribution.
- Nearby chemicals, PVC materials, oils or cleaning agents.

This map helps the supplier choose samples and packaging for the project. It also prevents a laboratory result from being treated as proof for an undefined real-world environment.

## Specify Protective Packing Clearly

Packaging should reduce moisture entry, carton crushing, contamination and direct exposure. The appropriate design depends on shipment size and route.

Common RFQ fields include individual wrapping, pack quantity, carton board grade, carton dimensions, pallet pattern, stretch wrapping, corner protection and moisture-control requirements. Do not request “waterproof packing” without defining the exposure. Protection from warehouse humidity is different from protection against standing water or outdoor rain.

Inspect packing during sample or pilot orders. A strong paper grade can still arrive unusable if rolls are compressed, cartons collapse or the wrapping opens during transport.

## Set Warehouse and Handling Rules

Keep thermal paper in its protective packing until needed. Store cartons away from direct sunlight, heaters, hot roofs, wet floors and incompatible chemicals. Use pallets or shelving rather than placing cartons directly on the floor.

Apply first-in, first-out stock rotation and keep batch labels visible. If rolls are moved from a very hot area into an air-conditioned room, allow controlled conditioning before opening packs when condensation is possible.

Avoid copying a universal temperature limit from an unrelated supplier page. Storage limits and image-life statements should refer to the selected grade's documentation and the project's acceptance method.

## Run a Conditioned Sample Test

Create a test that represents the buyer's most important risks without pretending to reproduce every shipping event.

1. Retain an untreated control sample.
2. Expose wrapped and unwrapped samples under recorded conditions.
3. Record temperature, humidity, duration and sample orientation.
4. Inspect background colour, curl, blocking and physical damage.
5. Print a standard test pattern on the actual printer.
6. Scan any barcode or QR elements and record the result.
7. Compare with the approved control and acceptance criteria.

For kitchen, parking or outdoor projects, add the relevant oil, splash, abrasion or light exposure separately. “Three-proof” should always be expanded into named protections and test conditions.

Our [thermal paper products](/products/thermal-paper-rolls) include options for different receipt and operating tasks. Grade selection should follow a documented exposure review and sample test.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Can thermal paper be stored in a shipping container?

It is routinely transported by container, but risk depends on route, season, duration, packing and paper grade. Buyers should review these conditions and approve shipment packaging rather than assume every container journey is equivalent.

### Does blackening mean the paper is permanently damaged?

Severe background darkening can reduce usable contrast and may make the roll unsuitable. Evaluate both the unprinted background and the print response against an approved sample.

### Is top-coated paper heat-proof?

No. A protective coating may improve selected resistance properties, but it does not create unlimited heat resistance. Confirm the named property, grade and test conditions.

Share your destination, route, storage period and point-of-use environment through our [quotation form](/contact) so packing and sample testing can be matched to the project.

title: How to Test QR Code Readability on Thermal Receipt Paper Before a Bulk Order
excerpt: A repeatable pre-production test for POS integrators and procurement teams evaluating QR contrast, module definition, printer settings, handling and aged samples.
metaTitle: How to Test QR Codes on Thermal Receipt Paper
metaDescription: Build a repeatable QR code receipt paper test using the real printer, invoice layout, scan devices, handling conditions and batch acceptance criteria.
metaKeywords: QR code test on receipt paper
content:
Test QR readability as a complete system: data, code size, POS layout, printer, settings, thermal paper and operating environment. A paper sample that looks dark is not automatically a reliable QR medium. Procurement teams need a repeatable test with recorded inputs and acceptance criteria before approving a bulk order.

## Build the Test Around the Real Receipt Workflow

Use the exact hardware and software planned for operation. A desktop sample printer may produce a different result from a high-speed store printer or compact payment terminal.

Record these inputs before testing:

- Printer brand, full model and firmware where available.
- Print speed, darkness or energy setting and driver configuration.
- Receipt width and printable area.
- QR dimensions, data payload and quiet-zone setting.
- Fonts, logos, rules and other content near the code.
- Paper grade, batch, GSM, dimensions and winding direction.
- Scanner or phone models used for verification.

For Saudi simplified tax invoices, QR generation belongs to the compliant invoicing solution. The [official ZATCA guidance](https://zatca.gov.sa/en/E-Invoicing/Introduction/Guidelines/Pages/default.aspx) should be checked for current system requirements. This paper test covers physical output and readability, not legal certification.

## Use a Test Matrix Instead of One Sample

Print enough receipts to expose variation. Include the smallest code, longest payload and busiest layout expected in production.

| Test stage | What to do | What to record |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Fresh print | Print at normal settings | Visual density and scan result |
| Setting variation | Test approved density range | Failure threshold and best setting |
| Repeat output | Print a batch of receipts | Consistency from first to last |
| Handling | Fold and lightly abrade samples | Damage and scan result |
| Environment | Store samples under defined conditions | Time, temperature, humidity and result |
| Batch comparison | Repeat with production lot | Match to approved reference |

Avoid uncontrolled tests such as leaving a receipt “somewhere hot” without recording conditions. They may reveal a problem, but they do not create a repeatable acceptance standard.

## Inspect More Than Whether the Phone Scans

A successful scan is essential, but visual inspection helps identify a process that is close to failure.

Look for:

1. Clear separation between dark and light modules.
2. No streaks, white lines or voids through the code.
3. No excessive dark spread that closes small gaps.
4. A clear quiet zone around the code.
5. No overlap from pre-printed artwork or background tint.
6. Consistent output across the roll and repeated prints.

Test with several common devices because camera focus and scanning software differ. Define the required scan distance and lighting condition for the buyer's operation. Do not promise a universal scan-rate percentage unless the test population and method are documented.

## Diagnose Failures in the Correct Order

When a code fails, do not assume the paper is the only cause. Check the source data and QR generation first, then layout, printer condition, settings and paper.

Common physical causes include a dirty or worn printhead, excessive print speed, low or excessive energy, poor roll contact, coating inconsistency, wrinkled paper and artwork interference. A damaged printer can make an approved paper appear defective. Conversely, changing settings to compensate for an unsuitable paper may create excess printhead heat or inconsistent results elsewhere.

Keep one approved roll and receipt as a control. When testing a new batch, print the control and candidate under the same settings.

## Convert the Test Into a Purchase Requirement

Attach the test method to the approval record. Identify the accepted paper grade and batch, printer model, settings, QR layout, sample date and reviewers. For custom rolls, attach the approved artwork revision.

The purchase specification should also state how production samples are selected. A useful plan checks samples across different cartons or parent rolls rather than only the first roll presented by the supplier.

Our [receipt paper roll range](/products/receipt-paper-rolls) can be qualified against the buyer's actual printer and QR layout. Request production only after the sample, artwork and test record agree.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Does darker thermal printing always improve QR readability?

No. Weak contrast can fail, but excessive dark spread can also reduce module definition. Use the normal printer and find a controlled setting that keeps edges clear.

### Can a supplier certify a QR scan rate without the buyer's system?

Not meaningfully. The result depends on the code, layout, printer, settings, paper, scanner and environment. The supplier can support a jointly defined test and provide batch evidence.

### Should custom artwork be approved before the QR test?

Yes. Background graphics, logos or fixed text can reduce the quiet zone or overlap variable fields. Test the final artwork and receipt layout together.

Send the printer model, current receipt and test requirements through our [contact page](/contact) to arrange a sample-based compatibility review.

title: Thermal Paper Roll Specification Checklist for Middle East Importers
excerpt: Use this RFQ checklist to compare thermal paper offers by dimensions, length, material, printing, packing, testing and delivery instead of headline price alone.
metaTitle: Thermal Paper RFQ Checklist for Middle East Buyers
metaDescription: A thermal paper roll RFQ checklist for Middle East importers covering width, diameter, core, length, GSM, custom printing, packing and sample approval.
content:
A reliable thermal paper RFQ should describe the physical roll, paper grade, printing requirement, packing method, inspection plan and destination. If the request says only “80mm thermal paper, best price,” suppliers may quote different lengths, cores, materials and carton configurations that cannot be compared fairly.

## Record the Physical Roll Specification

Use named fields and measurement units. Do not rely on a compact size label unless both parties define it in the same way.

| RFQ field | Example format | Why it matters |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Width | 80 mm | Matches the print mechanism |
| Outer diameter | Maximum 80 mm | Determines compartment fit |
| Core ID and OD | Millimetres | Affects loading and usable diameter |
| Roll length | Metres with tolerance | Enables value comparison |
| Paper weight | GSM | Influences thickness, feel and length |
| Winding | Coated side out or in | Places the thermal side against the head |
| End treatment | Glued, taped or loose | Affects final feeding and handling |

If the team does not know these values, measure an approved current roll and send it with the exact printer model. Our [thermal roll product pages](/products/thermal-paper-rolls) can help organize common options, but they do not replace device qualification.

## Define the Paper and Print Performance

“High quality” is not a testable specification. Describe what the receipt must do and where it will be used.

Useful qualification fields include:

- Printer model, speed and normal density setting.
- Required fresh print contrast and barcode or QR readability.
- Expected warehouse and point-of-use environment.
- Contact with oil, moisture, plasticizers, alcohol or abrasion.
- Required unprinted stock life and printed-image retention conditions.
- Material preferences such as BPA-free, BPS-free or phenol-free.
- Required declarations, test reports or batch documentation.

Do not treat top coating, high sensitivity, water resistance and long image life as interchangeable claims. Each describes a different property and needs its own grade-level evidence or project test.

## Specify Custom Printing as a Controlled Process

For Arabic-English or branded receipt rolls, add an artwork section to the RFQ. State whether the design is printed on the thermal face, back or both sides. Include colour count, repeat length, logo dimensions, fixed terms, variable-field clearance and version count.

The approval package should include:

1. Final artwork with fonts converted or supplied correctly.
2. A marked safe area for POS-generated text and QR codes.
3. Arabic-language review by the buyer or an authorized reviewer.
4. PDF proof showing winding and repeat direction.
5. Physical printed sample tested on the actual device.
6. Signed approval identifying the artwork revision.

Visit our [custom-printed thermal paper section](/products/thermal-paper-rolls/custom-printed) when the project includes logos, fixed text or private-label presentation.

## Compare Packing and Logistics, Not Just Roll Price

Middle East import projects may include long sea transit, container heat, handling and warehouse storage. The RFQ should state individual wrapping, rolls per pack, packs per carton, carton dimensions, gross weight, pallet requirement and any moisture or crush protection.

Also provide the destination port or delivery address and requested trade term. A supplier cannot compare FOB, CIF and delivered offers accurately without the destination and shipment size.

For mixed-SKU orders, define how each size will be identified. Carton labels should show the SKU, dimensions, quantity, production batch and any customer reference. This reduces receiving and distribution errors.

## Add an Inspection and Repeat-Order Plan

Sample approval is the beginning of control, not the end. Retain a signed master sample and purchase specification. Agree how width, diameter, core, length, print density, artwork position and packing will be checked.

For repeat orders, require a clear change-notification rule. A change in base paper, coating grade, core, ink, artwork or packing can affect device performance or customer acceptance. Batch traceability helps investigate complaints without assuming every roll is identical.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Why do two 80 x 80mm quotations have different prices?

They may use different roll lengths, GSM, cores, materials, tolerances or packing. Ask each supplier to complete the same specification sheet before comparing price.

### Should the RFQ specify both GSM and roll length?

Yes when both matter. GSM affects thickness and hand feel, while roll length defines usable paper. At a fixed outer diameter, changing thickness can change length.

### Is a printer model enough to approve the roll?

It is a strong starting point but not always sufficient. Device revisions, local settings and custom printing can change the result. Test an actual sample under the intended conditions.

Use our [quotation form](/contact) to submit the destination, device list, roll specification, annual volume and custom-printing files in one project brief.

title: 80mm vs 57mm Thermal Paper Rolls for GCC POS Systems
excerpt: Compare 80mm and 57mm receipt rolls by application, printer compartment, diameter, core, roll length and purchasing risk before placing a GCC bulk order.
metaTitle: 80mm vs 57mm Thermal Paper for GCC POS
metaDescription: Compare 80mm and 57mm thermal paper rolls for GCC retail POS, payment terminals and mobile printers, including diameter, core and testing requirements.
content:
Choose 80mm or 57mm thermal paper from the printer and buying task, not from the market name alone. An 80mm roll is commonly associated with full-size retail and restaurant receipt printers, while 57mm-class rolls are often used in compact payment terminals and mobile POS devices. Width alone, however, does not prove compatibility.

## Start With the Device and Application

The same GCC distributor may need both families. A supermarket checkout, hotel front desk, restaurant counter and handheld payment terminal solve different operational jobs.

| Buying situation | Likely starting family | Qualification priority |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Full-size retail POS receipt | 80mm | Width, outer diameter, length and printer setting |
| Restaurant billing printer | 80mm | Print density, order volume and storage environment |
| Countertop payment terminal | 57mm class | Exact model, paper compartment and core |
| Mobile POS terminal | 57mm class | Maximum diameter, tight winding and feeding stability |
| Kiosk or parking device | Project-specific | Drawing, black mark, winding and environmental exposure |

This table is a starting point, not a universal compatibility chart. A model-specific sample is more reliable than a general statement such as “fits all POS machines.”

## Understand What 80 x 80 and 57 x 40 Mean

Many buyers interpret a size such as 80 x 80 as width by outer diameter. Others may use different conventions or quote a nominal diameter. The purchase order should write every dimension by name.

For example:

- Width: 80 mm
- Maximum outer diameter: 80 mm
- Core inside diameter: project value
- Paper weight: project value in GSM
- Minimum roll length: measured or specified
- Coated side and winding direction: confirmed by sample

An 80mm roll with heavier paper may contain less length than a similar-diameter roll made from lighter paper. Comparing only carton price can therefore hide a material difference in usable length.

Our [80 x 80mm thermal roll page](/products/thermal-rolls/80x80mm) shows the type of specification structure buyers should use. Final values still need confirmation for the intended order.

## Treat 57mm as a Family, Not a Single SKU

The 57mm family includes several outer diameters and lengths. A compact terminal may accept a 57 x 30mm roll but not a 57 x 50mm roll, even though both share the same width. Core size and winding can also affect loading and feeding.

Before approving a 57mm order, collect:

1. Full printer or terminal brand and model.
2. Maximum paper compartment diameter.
3. Existing roll width, diameter and core.
4. A photo showing how the roll loads and unwinds.
5. Required receipts per roll or target roll length.
6. Whether the roll will carry pre-printed branding.

If a distributor supplies several terminal models, create a model-to-SKU matrix. This is safer than forcing one roll across the entire installed base.

## Compare Cost on a Usable Basis

For B2B purchasing, compare more than unit price. Use roll length, rolls per carton, carton dimensions, pallet quantity and expected waste or rejection risk. A lower-priced roll can become more expensive if it contains less paper, arrives crushed or causes loading complaints.

Ask suppliers to state measurement methods. Roll length can be measured directly or calculated from material thickness and roll geometry, but calculations depend on accurate inputs. For repeat orders, retain an approved reference sample and define acceptable tolerances in the purchase specification.

## Confirm the Roll With a Controlled Sample Test

Run the sample on the actual device under normal operating conditions. Check loading, cover closure, feed, cut, print density and end-of-roll behaviour. Print several long receipts, not only a short test line. For QR receipts, include the smallest code and most detailed layout used by the business.

When the project will operate in hot vehicles, kiosks or non-air-conditioned storage, add an environment-specific review. Standard indoor approval does not prove outdoor or high-heat performance.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Are all 80mm thermal paper rolls interchangeable?

No. Width may match while diameter, core, paper weight, length, winding or grade differs. Confirm each field and test the intended printer.

### Can one 57mm roll fit every payment terminal?

No. Paper compartments and core requirements vary by model. Use the exact terminal model and maximum outer diameter during qualification.

### Which size is better for a GCC distributor to stock?

Stock should follow the installed printer base and customer mix. Many distributors need multiple 80mm and 57mm SKUs. Sales history and device mapping should decide the range.

Send your model list and current roll samples through our [contact page](/contact) to build a practical size and replenishment matrix.

title: How to Choose Thermal Paper for GCC VAT Receipts and QR Code Printing
excerpt: A practical purchasing guide for GCC retailers, POS integrators and paper distributors evaluating receipt clarity, QR readability, roll specifications and custom printing.
metaTitle: Thermal Paper for GCC VAT & QR Receipts
metaDescription: Learn how GCC buyers should specify thermal paper for VAT receipts, QR readability, POS compatibility, hot-climate storage and Arabic-English printing.
content:
Thermal paper does not make an invoice legally compliant by itself. Compliance starts with the invoicing system, required invoice data and the generated QR code. The paper buyer's job is to make sure the selected roll fits the printer, produces readable output under the intended settings and remains usable through transport, storage and daily operation.

## Separate Invoice Compliance From Paper Performance

This distinction prevents one of the most common sourcing errors. A supplier can provide consistent receipt paper, but the supplier cannot certify that a retailer's tax invoice workflow is compliant merely because a QR code prints on the roll.

For Saudi projects, the current requirements should be checked against the [official ZATCA e-invoicing guidance](https://zatca.gov.sa/en/E-Invoicing/Introduction/Guidelines/Pages/default.aspx). ZATCA explains that simplified tax invoices use QR codes generated by the electronic invoicing solution. Your tax adviser or POS solution provider should confirm the applicable invoice fields and system requirements.

The paper qualification should focus on a different set of questions:

- Does the roll fit the exact printer model and paper compartment?
- Does the printer produce a sharp QR code at its normal speed and energy setting?
- Are variable fields kept clear of any pre-printed logo, terms or background design?
- Can the roll tolerate the expected warehouse, vehicle and store conditions?
- Can the supplier reproduce the same paper, dimensions and packing on repeat orders?

## Define the Receipt Roll Before Requesting a Quote

Do not request only an “80mm VAT roll.” The width is just one part of the specification.

| Specification | Why it matters | What the buyer should provide |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Width | Must match the print mechanism | Width in millimetres |
| Outer diameter | Must fit the paper compartment | Maximum accepted diameter |
| Core | Affects fit and remaining roll space | Core inside and outside diameter |
| Roll length | Affects receipt yield and price comparison | Required or minimum length |
| Paper weight | Changes thickness, feel and length at a fixed diameter | Target GSM or approved sample |
| Winding | Determines which coated side faces the thermal head | Coated side and unwind direction |
| Packing | Protects rolls during export and storage | Rolls per pack, carton and pallet |

If these fields are unavailable, send the printer model, a photo of the compartment and a physical reference roll. Our [thermal paper roll range](/products/thermal-paper-rolls) provides a starting point, but a sample test remains the final qualification step.

## Test QR Readability Under Real Operating Conditions

A QR code that scans once from a fresh laboratory print is not enough. Use the actual printer model, production software and intended invoice layout. Print multiple samples at the normal store speed and density setting, then scan them with more than one common phone or business scanner.

The test should include:

1. Fresh output at the normal printing setting.
2. Output after normal handling, folding and light abrasion.
3. Aged samples stored under the project's expected conditions.
4. The smallest QR code and densest receipt layout expected in operation.
5. Samples from the beginning and end of the roll.

Record the printer model, settings, paper batch and acceptance result. This turns “clear printing” into a repeatable approval method rather than a subjective claim.

## Plan for GCC Storage and Transport

Heat exposure can activate or darken thermal coating, while prolonged heat, light, moisture and chemical contact can reduce printed-image stability. The risk depends on the paper grade, exposure level and duration. Avoid universal temperature or image-life promises unless they are supported by a named grade and test conditions.

For an export project, confirm individual wrapping, carton strength, pallet protection, container loading plan and warehouse practices. Keep cartons away from heat sources, direct sunlight, water and incompatible chemicals. If the rolls will be stored in vehicles, outdoor kiosks or hot back rooms, disclose that use before sample approval.

## Decide Whether Arabic-English Pre-Printing Is Needed

The POS system usually prints transaction-specific data. Pre-printing is better suited to stable elements such as a logo, customer-service information, campaign text or fixed terms. Before production, separate variable system fields from fixed artwork.

Arabic artwork requires native-language review. Check right-to-left direction, connected letter forms, punctuation, number order and alignment. Approve a PDF proof and a physical printed sample, not only an editable design file.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Does QR code receipt paper make a Saudi invoice compliant?

No. The invoicing solution and invoice data determine compliance. Paper selection supports physical print quality and readability. Always verify current requirements with official authorities and qualified advisers.

### Is high-sensitivity paper always better for QR codes?

Not automatically. Sensitivity must be matched with the printer's energy, speed and environment. Excessive density can close small QR modules, while insufficient density can create weak contrast. Test the complete printer-paper-layout combination.

### Should GCC buyers choose 60 or 65 GSM paper?

GSM affects thickness, hand feel and possible roll length, but it is not a quality grade by itself. Confirm the paper compartment, desired length and sample performance before choosing a heavier paper.

For a project-specific review, send your printer model, target market, roll dimensions, storage conditions and sample receipt through our [quotation form](/contact).

title: Best Thermal Paper Manufacturers in China (2026 Buyer's Guide)
excerpt: A factual 2026 comparison of leading thermal paper roll & label manufacturers in China — certifications, MOQ, OEM capability, and export reach to help distributors source right.
content:
China supplies the majority of the world's thermal paper rolls and labels. For distributors, importers, and OEM buyers, the hard part isn't finding a factory — it's finding one with the **certifications, consistency, and export support** your accounts depend on. This guide compares the leading thermal paper manufacturers in China in 2026, with the selection criteria that actually matter for wholesale buyers.

## How We Evaluated Manufacturers

A reliable thermal paper supplier should clear all of these:

- **Certifications**: ISO 9001 (quality system), BPA-free / phenol-free formulations, FSC (sustainable sourcing)
- **Consistency**: stable image density, roll length, and core size across repeat shipments
- **OEM / private label**: custom sizing, coating grade, core printing, branded packaging
- **Export readiness**: full documentation (CO, packing list, BL), CIF/FOB/DDP terms, fast container loading
- **Communication**: fast quoting and English-speaking sales support

## Leading Thermal Paper Manufacturers in China (2026)

### ZhixinPaper (Xi'an Zhi Xin Paper Co., Ltd.)

Founded 2009 in Xi'an. ISO 9001, BPA-free and FSC-certified thermal paper rolls and labels, with dedicated OEM/private-label service and export to 80+ countries. A strong fit for distributors needing consistent repeat orders plus regional packaging (Arabic, Turkish, and more) and CIF pricing to the Middle East, Africa, and Southeast Asia.

*Best for: OEM/private label, geo-focused distributors (MEA/SEA), mixed-size pallet orders.*

### Sailing Paper (Shenzhen Sailing Paper Products Co., Ltd.)

Established around 2005, OEM/ODM capable with factory-direct pricing, exporting to 100+ countries and operating overseas warehouses in markets including the US, Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Nigeria.

*Best for: buyers wanting a large established catalogue and overseas stock.*

### Panda Paper Roll (Shandong Ultra Paper Co., Ltd.)

Founded 2000, ISO 9001 and FSC certified, exporting to 60+ countries with high monthly container throughput.

*Best for: high-volume container buyers.*

### Xiandai Paper (Suzhou Xiandai Paper Production Co., Ltd.)

Founded 1983; one of China's largest and longest-running thermal paper converters.

*Best for: very large-scale jumbo-roll converting.*

### Tele-Paper (Oji Group)

A long-established BPA-free thermal paper specialist exporting to 130+ countries; a subsidiary of Japan's Oji Group.

*Best for: buyers prioritizing brand-name pedigree.*

## Quick Comparison

| Manufacturer | Since | Key certifications | OEM / private label | Export reach |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## How to Choose the Right Supplier for Your Market

- **POS / receipt rolls** (80x80mm, 57x50mm): confirm core size and length match your customers' terminals.
- **Shipping / barcode labels** (4x6): confirm adhesive type and printer compatibility.
- **Regulated markets** (EU/US): require BPA-free / phenol-free lab reports (SGS or Intertek, under 12 months old).
- **Branded resale**: choose a supplier with real private-label packaging, not just stickers.

Sourcing for the Middle East, Africa, or Southeast Asia? See our [market guides](/markets) for CIF terms and in-stock sizes by region, or [request a quote](/contact) for a same-week price list.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Who is the best thermal paper manufacturer in China?**

There's no single "best" — it depends on your volume, market, and customization needs. For OEM/private label and regional export support, ZhixinPaper is a strong fit; for very large jumbo-roll converting, older converters like Xiandai scale further.

**What certifications should a thermal paper supplier have?**

At minimum ISO 9001, plus BPA-free / phenol-free formulations and ideally FSC. For EU/US resale, require recent third-party lab reports.

**What's a typical MOQ for OEM thermal paper?**

Standard sizes often start around 1 pallet; fully custom formulations or printed packaging require larger runs. ZhixinPaper offers MOQ from 1 pallet on core sizes. See [OEM & custom printing](/oem).

**Can I get private-label or custom-printed rolls?**

Yes — leading manufacturers offer custom width, core, coating grade, core printing, and branded packaging.
